behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (summary write path must read stored findings), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: verdict comes from stored records, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain required. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: could be Unit if both inputs are already in-memory in one function. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration wins because bug appears when persistence/query + summary-save logic are wired; would flip to Unit if behavior text said check is a pure function over provided findings. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about "stored dimension finding" and "blocked when any ... FAIL," not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary persistence service to findings repository (real local DB or equivalent in-process repo) and attempt to store PASS summary for a round containing at least one FAIL finding; assert write is rejected.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: defect occurs if gate module consumes wrong data source (summary text) instead of findings store. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed. LLM-DEP: correctness is source-of-truth selection, not LLM output. MULTI-STEP: single gate computation step. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: might be Unit if gate function directly receives both objects and no repository call exists. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration wins because real failure boundary is gate↔storage wiring; would flip to Unit if behavior explicitly constrained to a pure selector function. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic since behavior says "reads ... stored findings directly" and compares against "hand-written summary text."
behaviors.test_description: Execute gate computation with repository-backed findings containing a FAIL while summary text says PASS; assert gate returns non-PASS based on stored findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires coordination of write API, authorization/flag check, and audit record persistence. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local persistence; no OS/network/subprocess requirement in text. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: not a multi-agent sequence. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: could be Unit if warning is just a returned struct and no persistence. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration wins because "auditable warning record" implies durable cross-module effect; would flip to Unit if audit output were only an in-memory return value. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require external logging service or OS primitive.
behaviors.test_description: Call summary-override endpoint/service without override flag and assert rejection; call with flag and assert summary is accepted plus a persisted warning/audit entry tied to actor and round.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in commit-to-review-round linkage across modules (fix metadata, review rounds, gate). REAL-INFRA: real git subprocess is not explicitly required; commit ids/timestamps can be modeled in-process. LLM-DEP: requirement is freshness ordering, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: sequence exists but can be checked as data dependency, not full agent pipeline. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: could be System if actual git history/merge-base behavior is necessary to reproduce ordering bugs. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration wins because behavior asks for identifier ordering rule; would flip to System if text required reading commit ancestry via real git commands in test. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text requires one post-fix review identifier check, not multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Create fix-commit metadata and review-round records, then attempt PASS with stale (pre-fix) round id and with fresh (post-fix) round id; assert only fresh id allows PASS path.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a fake runner can claim a round ran, hiding failures where real review-agent execution on updated diff is skipped/broken. REAL-INFRA: external model/API execution path is part of behavior wording ("live review loop", "review-agent round runs"). LLM-DEP: behavior requires real agent run occurrence tied to updated diff; this is LLM-involved stage orchestration. MULTI-STEP: primarily one required fresh agentic step before finalize, so Agentic over Workflow. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: could be Integration if test only validates timestamp/round-id gating and treats agent execution as a boolean event. TOP2-CONTRAST: Agentic wins because this behavior explicitly requires a real agent round in live flow; would flip to Integration if text said "simulate agent completion event" or explicitly excluded real model calls. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because text requires one fresh round condition, not a full multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live loop against an updated diff with real review-agent invocation enabled; verify PASS finalization is denied until a post-update agent round actually executes and records results.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires rounds counter/state plus stored findings plus decision policy interacting correctly. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP: uses stored MUST-FIX results; does not require judging model output quality in test assertion. MULTI-STEP: policy evaluation at exhaustion point, not necessarily full live chain. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: could be Workflow if reproducing bug needs real multi-round agent execution history. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration wins because behavior is phrased over "stored results" and exhaustion state; would flip to Workflow if text required validating decisions across actual sequential agent rounds. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text anchors on "stored results" and escalation policy, not model decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Set up round-state as exhausted and persist unresolved MUST-FIX findings; execute final decision computation and assert escalation outcome and PASS denial.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can emerge only across sequential round transitions plus final gate/elevation behavior. REAL-INFRA: may include live services, but core boundary here is multi-step orchestration. LLM-DEP: live review workflow implies agentic rounds; handoff correctness depends on stage outputs across rounds. MULTI-STEP: explicitly yes ("live multi-round review workflow"). ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: could be Agentic if only one model decision effectively determines final state and prior rounds are synthetic. TOP2-CONTRAST: Workflow wins because requirement is about exhaustion behavior across multiple rounds and final escalation emission; would flip to Agentic if scenario were reduced to a single fresh round plus final gate check. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due behavior text explicitly requiring "live multi-round ... workflow," which disqualifies single-boundary integration coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end live multi-round review pipeline where MUST-FIX findings remain unresolved until round budget is exhausted; assert PASS remains blocked and escalation signal/record is emitted at workflow end.
